title: Harvard grad student workers end 40-day strike without a contract
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Harvard Graduate Student Union ended a 40-day strike without a contract, citing progress in negotiations with the university.
The union represents over 4,000 student workers and will resume talks with Harvard on June 9 and 23.
Harvard proposed a 2.75% raise upon contract ratification, plus additional benefits for part-time student workers.

title: A.J. Brown trade grades: Did experts think Patriots gave up too much for star WR?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots acquired A.J. Brown from the Eagles for a 2028 first-round pick and a 2027 fifth-round pick.
Experts gave mixed grades for the trade, with concerns about Brown's age and past performance affecting opinions.
Philadelphia received positive evaluations for adding draft picks despite potential weaknesses in their wide receiver group.

title: 7News anchor leaving the station after 6 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Crawford to leave WHDH-TV's 7News after nearly six years, announced on social media.
Crawford, a TV journalist for 17 years, plans career transition for more flexibility.
She has reported on diverse events, from presidential debates to natural disasters, during her career.

title: 3 ways A.J. Brown will likely impact Patriots offense, Drake Maye in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots acquired All-Pro wideout A.J. Brown from Eagles in exchange for 2028 first-round and 2027 fifth-round picks.
A.J. Brown's ability to beat man coverage could enhance QB Drake Maye's performance against defenses he excels against.
Brown's acquisition may lead to changes in Patriots' receiving corps, potentially impacting players like Kayshon Boutte.

title: Norton bakery apologizes for customer-requested Hitler cake posted on Facebook
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Norton's Sweet Stuff Bake Shop apologized for a cake with "South Park" character Cartman dressed as Hitler.
The bakery faced backlash after posting the customer-requested cake photo on Facebook, later removing the post.
Community reaction to the apology was mixed, with criticism, support, and calls for a more serious response.

title: How windshields can affect tinted eyeglasses
author: John Paul
bullets: Photochromic lenses don't tint inside cars because modern windshields filter out UV light needed to activate the lenses.
Windshields are laminated to provide UV protection, structural rigidity, roof-crush protection, and airbag support.
No modern vehicles allow UV light through the windshield, preventing glasses from tinting inside the car.

title: Mass. police department cancels appearance of Trump-backed ‘Freedom Truck’ over ties to right-wing organization
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Westfield Police Department canceled 'Freedom Truck' visit due to its political affiliations with conservative PragerU and Freedom 250.
The 'Freedom Truck' mobile museum was set to stop at Westfield State University June 14-16 for community engagement.
Cancellation follows similar withdrawals from Freedom 250 events, with artists like Martina McBride and Bret Michaels pulling out.

title: DA: Man charged in Pittsfield fatal stabbing had ‘no prior connection’ to victim
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Zyrus Jaynes, 24, charged with murder for fatally stabbing Jesse Gray, 36, in Pittsfield on May 29.
Jaynes allegedly had an altercation with Gray before stabbing him; they had no prior connection.
Jaynes, previously arraigned on assault charges, pleaded not guilty and is held without bail.

title: Patriots lose new tight end for 2026 season due to injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Julian Hill, signed by the Patriots in March, will miss the 2026 season due to injury.
Hill was expected to be the backup tight end behind Hunter Henry for New England.
Eli Raridon, the 2026 third-round pick, may see an increased role in Hill's absence.

title: 5 clues A.J. Brown dropped about his desire to join the Patriots
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown had a long-standing interest in the Patriots, being a fan and expressing disappointment when they didn't draft him in 2019.
Brown changed his Instagram profile picture to Tom Brady in 2024, citing nostalgia and admiration for the Patriots quarterback.
In a podcast with Julian Edelman, Brown hinted at joining the Patriots, sharing a wink and excitement over a Brady-signed jersey.
